title: Supreme Court Hears Case on California's Vehicle Emissions EPA Waiver
content: The Supreme Court heard oral argument in Diamond Alternative Energy, LLC v. Environmental Protection Agency, a case examining whether fuel companies may challenge an EPA waiver that allows California to set its own auto emissions standards. The companies argued for their right to sue the EPA, explaining their business interests were harmed by the waiver. The U.S. Court of Appeals for the D.C. Circuit ruled that the fuel companies lacked standing, and this appeal followed.
author: Analysts Account
